short_term.title: Short-term view (0–30 days · next earnings cycle)
bullets: Run-rate $27.00B in FY2025 Q4, +10.8% YoY — accelerating trajectory entering the next print.
Next-quarter consensus: $24.50B (-9.3% vs current Q) — set by 21 broker contributors.
EPS consensus next qtr: $1.85.
Margin context: 5.7% net margin (7.8% op margin) — compressed — watch operating leverage.
Next earnings: 2026-08-27 — primary catalyst for the short-term window.
short_term.takeaway: Going into the next earnings print, the setup is mixed — execution risk on the next guide is elevated.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ days · multi-year setup)
bullets: TTM revenue $105B — in cyclical drawdown.
Sell-side: FY1 $98B → FY2 $110B (+12.2% multi-year top-line implied).
Forward EBIT margin: 28.0% — structural premium business over the long-cycle view.
6-quarter run-rate CAGR: +16.8% — the secular trajectory the market is pricing.
Sector thesis (Information Technology): Multi-year AI/cloud capex cycle remains the dominant secular theme.
long_term.takeaway: Long-cycle thesis is intact if the next 2–4 quarters confirm a stabilization in trajectory.

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–45 DTE · front-month tactical)
bullets: Front-month ATM IV: 109.9% — extreme (binary-event hedging implied).
Put/Call OI: 0.73 — balanced flow.
30-day implied move: ±20.4% ($234.88 – $355.50) — the 1-σ range the options market is pricing.
ATM skew: +7.59pp — put skew (downside hedges bid).
Front-month OI cluster: $260.0 — likely magnet / pin-risk for expiry.
short_term.strategy: Sell 30–45 DTE cash-secured put at $280 (5% OTM). High IV (110%) + uptrend → collect premium with a willingness to be assigned at a discount. Roll if breached, otherwise let theta decay.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ DTE · LEAPS / structural)
bullets: Back-month avg IV (90+ DTE): 68.2% across 9 expiries.
Available long-dated expiries: 9 dates spanning 91–609 DTE.
90-day implied move: ±35.2% ($191.26 – $399.12).
391-day implied move: ±67.9% ($94.69 – $495.69) — the long-dated 1-σ range.
Sell-side PT vs options-implied range: consensus $150 (-49.2% upside). Compare to the 90+ DTE implied range above.
Term structure in backwardation (-41.7pp front→back) — near-term event premium; long-dated vol cheaper.
long_term.strategy: Sell 6-month strangle: short $266 put / short $325 call. Range-bound + high IV → premium-selling on both sides; profits if spot stays within ±10%; manage with rolls or assignment.
